intro: Piper adenandrum, also known as Long Pepper Vine, is a type of flowering plant in the family Piperaceae, native to Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Piper adenandrum include alkaloids, such as piperadendrin and piperenol, as well as phenolic compounds and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the Long Pepper Vine is commonly prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is often used in combination with other herbs to treat various health conditions.
